# BYD Sees 33% Profit Jump In Q2 2024 As Global EV Expansion Accelerates

BYD Delivered Nearly 1 Million NEVs In Q2 2024

[Michael Phoon](/news/author/michaelphoon)

August 28, 2024| Updated 09:19, August 28, 2024

2 min read

![cover image](/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fwp-blog.ev.com%2Fwp-content%2Fuploads%2F2024%2F05%2FBYD_Blog-4.png&w=3840&q=75)

![cover image](/_next/image?url=https%3A%2F%2Fwp-blog.ev.com%2Fwp-content%2Fuploads%2F2024%2F05%2FBYD_Blog-4.png&w=3840&q=75)

BYD has reported a remarkable financial performance for the second quarter of 2024, demonstrating its resilience in the face of intense competition and aggressive pricing strategies in the global EV market.

### Record-Breaking Sales and Profits

BYD **[announced a net income of 9.06 billion yuan ($1.27 billion) for Q2 2024](https://www.bydglobal.com/sitesresources/common/tools/generic/web/viewer.html?file=%2Fsites%2FSatellite%2FBYD%20PDF%20Viewer%3Fblobcol%3Durldata%26blobheader%3Dapplication%252Fpdf%26blobkey%3Did%26blobtable%3DMungoBlobs%26blobwhere%3D1638928476298%26ssbinary%3Dtrue)**, marking a 33% year-over-year increase and nearly doubling by up to 98.36% from Q1 2024. This represents the company’s second-highest quarterly profit on record, surpassed only by Q3 2023.

The impressive financial results were driven by record-breaking vehicle sales. BYD delivered 986,720 new energy vehicles (NEVs), including both EVs and hybrids, in Q2 2024, which is a 40.25% increase year-over-year and a 57.56% jump from Q1 2024. Revenue for the quarter reached 176.2 billion yuan ($24.7 billion), up 25.89% from the previous year and 41.01% from Q1 2024.

Despite the overall strong performance, BYD’s gross margin slipped to 18.69% in Q2, down from 21.88% in Q1 2024. This decline reflects the ongoing price war in China’s EV market, where BYD has been offering significant discounts on its bestselling Dynasty and Ocean series EVs to maintain its market leadership.

However, for the first half of 2024, **[BYD’s gross margin actually improved to 20.01%](https://www.bydglobal.com/sitesresources/common/tools/generic/web/viewer.html?file=%2Fsites%2FSatellite%2FBYD%20PDF%20Viewer%3Fblobcol%3Durldata%26blobheader%3Dapplication%252Fpdf%26blobkey%3Did%26blobtable%3DMungoBlobs%26blobwhere%3D1638928476298%26ssbinary%3Dtrue)**, up from 18.33% in the same period last year. The company attributed this increase to the growth of its NEV business.

### BYD’s Aggressive Global Expansion

BYD is aggressively expanding its global presence. Stella Li, Executive Vice President, stated that the company aims for “nearly half” of its sales to come from overseas markets in the future. BYD is on track to **[reach 500,000 overseas sales in 2024](https://fortune.com/asia/2024/08/28/byd-nearly-half-sales-from-overseas-china-ev-new-factories/#)**.

BYD Thailand Plant | Inside the All-New World-Leading Production Line (Video BYD)

To support this growth, BYD is establishing manufacturing facilities across the globe including a manufacturing plant **[recently opened in Thailand with other plants planned](https://www.ev.com/news/byd-expands-global-footprint-with-first-southeast-asian-ev-plant-in-thailand&blogId=3632)** or under construction in Mexico, Hungary, Brazil, Turkey, and Pakistan. There are also ongoing discussions about entering the Canadian market despite the country recently announcing to implement 100% tariff rate on Chinese EVs and also **[new tariff rates for them in Europe](https://ev.com/news/eu-revises-tariffs-on-chinese-evs-with-teslas-rate-slashed-to-9&blogId=4925)**.

**Read More: [Canada Announces 100% Tariff On Chinese EV To Shield Its Automotive Industry](https://ev.com/news/canada-announces-100-tariff-on-chinese-ev-to-shield-its-automotive-industry&blogId=5074)**

Notably, BYD is not just focusing on vehicle sales. The company has **[announced a partnership with Uber to deploy 100,000 new EVs for its drivers](https://ev.com/news/uber-partners-with-cruise-to-launch-self-driving-ev-rides-in-2025&blogId=5024)**. The collaboration also aims to bring autonomous vehicle technology to Uber’s global platform.

### BYD’s Global Market Position

BYD has secured a dominant position in China’s NEV market with over one-third market share. The company has outsold the combined sales of Volkswagen Group’s two joint ventures in China by [**14.5% in the first seven months of 2024**.](https://ca.finance.yahoo.com/news/china-electric-vehicle-giant-byds-104238604.html#:~:text=BYD%20has%20outsold%20the%20combined,according%20to%20Counterpoint%20Research%20estimates.)

As BYD continues to expand globally and invest in advanced technologies, it appears well-positioned to maintain its strong growth trajectory, despite the challenges of intense competition and international trade barriers. Nevertheless, the company’s growth has not wavered as it accelerate EV adoption worldwide.
